name: First World War
summary: Imperialist world war that produced unprecedented destruction and helped generate the revolutionary wave of 1917-23.

name: Zimmerwald Conference
summary: Gathering of anti-war socialists opposing the First World War.

name: Easter Rising
summary: Armed uprising against British rule in Ireland.

name: February Revolution
summary: Mass strikes and demonstrations overthrew Tsar Nicholas II and opened a revolutionary period.

name: April Theses
summary: Lenin argued for all power to the soviets and opposition to the Provisional Government.

name: Russian Workers State
summary: The Russian working class seized power in October and established the USSR

name: Stalinist Counter Revolution
summary: The stalinist bureaucracy forms out of the destruction of the Russian workers state and centralises power.

name: July Days
summary: Armed demonstrations by workers and soldiers in Petrograd.

name: Kornilov Affair
summary: Attempted military coup that strengthened support for the Bolsheviks.

name: October Revolution
summary: Insurrection led by the Bolsheviks that transferred power to the soviets.

name: Russian Civil War
summary: Civil war between the Soviet government and counter-revolutionary forces.

name: Finnish Civil War
summary: Conflict between Red and White forces following Finnish independence.

name: German Revolution
summary: Mass uprising of workers and soldiers that overthrew the Kaiser and ended the war.

name: Spartacist Uprising
summary: Revolutionary uprising in Berlin associated with the Spartacist movement.

name: Murder of Rosa Luxemburg and Karl Liebknecht
summary: Leading revolutionaries were murdered by Freikorps forces.

name: Hungarian Soviet Republic
summary: Short-lived workers' state established during the post-war revolutionary wave.

name: Bavarian Soviet Republic
summary: Short-lived workers' republic established in Bavaria.

name: Biennio Rosso
summary: Wave of strikes, factory occupations and social unrest that brought Italy close to revolution.

name: Italian Factory Occupations
summary: Hundreds of thousands of workers occupied factories during the height of the Biennio Rosso.

name: Polish-Soviet War
summary: War between Soviet Russia and Poland during the revolutionary period.

name: Kapp Putsch
summary: Right-wing coup attempt defeated by a mass general strike.

name: March Action
summary: Major confrontation between German workers and the state.

name: Kronstadt Rebellion
summary: Revolt of sailors during the final stages of the Russian Civil War.

name: March on Rome
summary: Fascist seizure of power led by Benito Mussolini.

name: German October
summary: Failed revolutionary attempt during the German crisis of 1923.

name: Chinese Revolution Begins
summary: Mass workers' and anti-imperialist movement culminating in the revolutionary struggles of 1925-27.

name: Shanghai Massacre
summary: Chiang Kai-shek's forces massacred communists and workers in Shanghai.

name: British General Strike
summary: Nationwide strike involving millions of workers.
